items.hook: Jackass: Best and Last review – kings of gross-out comedy’s final, funny farewell
items.body:
So-called final outing for Johnny Knoxville and his daring, stunt-hungry pals might be close to a greatest hits reel, but there are enough laughs to warrant the nostalgia

items.hook: Florida Roadkill is a black comedy crime novel by Tim Dorsey, the first in his series centered around the character Serge A.
items.body: It was published in 1999 by William Morrow and Company, an imprint of HarperCollins. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: ‘His chords are like sheets of diamond ice’: the beguiling psych-folk of late cult songwriter Ed Askew
items.body:
The late songwriter released around 50 records, writing gay love songs and effortless folk songs from folk’s fringes. Fans including Bill Callahan explain his singular brilliance

items.hook: Social Darwinists to tech bros: tracing the long fight against equality in US
items.body:
Historian Kim Phillips-Fein’s book Country of Lords looks back at the many men who have tried to fight against egalitarianism, from John Adams to Peter Thiel

items.hook: Kingdom of Prussia re-conquers Mainz from France.
items.body: The Kingdom of Prussia was a German state that existed from 1701 to 1918. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Little Brother review – Netflix comedy is neither weird or funny enough for star Eric André
items.body:
The surreal comedian struggles to sell a middling and mostly conventional film about an uptight realtor whose life is upturned by an unpredictable figure from his past

items.hook: Jackie Robinson becomes the first African American to be inducted into the National Baseball Hall of Fame.
items.body: Jack Roosevelt Robinson was an American professional baseball player who was the first African American to play in Major League Baseball (MLB) in the modern era. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: A Bell for Adano is a 1944 novel by John Hersey, the winner of the 1945 Pulitzer Prize for Fiction.
items.body: It tells the story of an Italian-American officer in Sicily during World War II who wins the respect and admiration of the people of the town of Adano by helping them find a replacement for the town bell that the Fascists had melted down for rifle barrels. Wor

items.body:
1974 book led to making of seminal documentary and introduced Arnold Schwarzenegger to the world

items.hook: Godzilla vs Kong actor Kaylee Hottle dies aged 18
items.body:
The deaf actor, who played a major role in Godzilla vs Kong and its 2024 sequel, was killed in a car crash in Maryland

items.hook: The 100 best novels of all time
items.body: The top 100 novels of all time published in English, as voted for by authors, critics and academics worldwide. How many have you read?

items.hook: Megawati Sukarnoputri was sworn in as the first female president of Indonesia following her predecessor's impeachment.
items.body: Diah Permata Megawati Setiawati Sukarnoputri is an Indonesian politician who served as the fifth president of Indonesia from 2001 to 2004 and the eighth vice president under President Abdurrahman Wahid from 1999 to 2001. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Strangers and Brothers is a series of novels by C.
items.body: Snow, published between 1940 and 1970. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Election of pope John V following the death of pope Benedict II two months prior.
items.body: Pope John V was the bishop of Rome from 23 July 685 to his death on 2 August 686. One of those things you can't un-know.

Abkhazia, officially the Republic of Abkhazia, is a partially recognised state in the South Caucasus, which is internationally recognized by the most states as part of Georgia. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Carole Berry is an American mystery fiction writer who is best known for her amateur sleuth series featuring New York City office temp worker Bonnie Indermill.
items.body: Berry also has one suspense novel to her credit, titled Nightmare Point. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: ANA Flight 61 is hijacked in Tokyo, Japan by Yuji Nishizawa.
items.body: All Nippon Airways Flight 61 was a scheduled domestic flight from Tokyo Haneda Airport in Ōta, Tokyo, Japan and was en route to New Chitose Airport in Chitose, Japan, near Sapporo. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Catharine Jan Morris was a Welsh historian, author and travel writer.
items.body: She was known particularly for the Pax Britannica trilogy (1968–1978), a history of the British Empire, and for portraits of cities, including Oxford, Venice, Trieste, Hong Kong and New York City. One of those things you can't un-know.
